title: atriplex glauca subsp. ifniensis
intro: Atriplex glauca subsp. ifniensis, also known as the Ifni orache, is a subspecies of the Atriplex glauca plant, which is a type of succulent shrub native to the Canary Islands and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Atriplex glauca subsp. ifniensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as arthritis and bronchitis. The plant is typically harvested and prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or dried powder, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
